title: Festive Crunchy Moroccan Cauliflower Stir-fry
description: A festive, crunchy Moroccan dish featuring fermented cauliflower tossed with Thai red curry paste for a touch of Middle Eastern flair, suitable for nut-free diets.
instructions: Wash the cauliflower thoroughly, remove the tough outer layer, and cut into florets. Soak in salted water for at least 30 minutes to help ferment.
Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium heat with 1 tablespoon of oil. Add the chopped garlic and sauté until fragrant but not browned.
Add red curry paste, stir-fry for about 2 minutes until it releases its flavor into the mixture. Pour in coconut milk gradually while continuously stirring to prevent sticking.
Once the cauliflower has fermented enough (usually within 1-2 days), drain and pat dry. Add to skillet with remaining oil. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
Sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds, cumin seeds, red chili flakes, and salt according to your taste preferences. Cook for another 5 minutes until cauliflower is tender yet crispy.
Drizzle with lime juice a few seconds before removing from heat. Serve hot as a snack or side dish.
